NORTHROOT
ProductSystemsDocsJournalExamples

Section

ProductSystemsDocsJournalExamples
Product

What Northroot Is Not

Boundary notes for what the trust kernel does not currently provide.

Northroot’s public kernel is deliberately narrow. It covers evidence identity, journal framing, and offline verification.

It does not currently provide:

  • a hosted control plane
  • an operating console
  • an access-control product
  • an agent planning system
  • a workflow engine
  • a customer-data store
  • a policy decision runtime

Those may become separate systems later. They are not the current public kernel.

Implementation Notes

Public pages should describe current primitives and verified examples. Internal tools, operating consoles, and sign-in paths stay outside the public navigation until they exist as real software.

Operational Lessons

Trust comes from coherence and restraint. The site should show the shape of the system without presenting future systems as current capabilities.

Source

Explanations live here. Canonical contracts remain in source.
